01_initial_request.input_data: Cryo-EM structural data and existing literature
01_initial_request.user_intent: To provide an overview of how cryo-EM techniques facilitate drug discovery

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cryo-EM resolution had hardly resolved structures within 4 Å before 2013. [Gap] True atomic resolution was needed to rival X-ray crystallography for drug discovery. [Decision] To achieve atomic resolution, chose to use Cryo-EM with direct electron detectors to perform single-particle reconstruction on apoferritin, expecting to obtain 1.2 Å structure.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cryo-EM structure of apoferritin at 1.2 Å resolution was reported, marking the development of this technology to a real atomic resolution level.

references.citation: Yip et al. [34] and Nakane et al. [35]
references.relevance: Reports independent achievement of 1.2 Å resolution using cryo-EM.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GPCRs are targeted by 30% of drugs but difficult to crystallize in activated conformations. [Gap] Structural information on activated GPCR conformations for SBDD was missing. [Decision] To facilitate SBDD for GLP-1R, chose to use Cryo-EM to perform structure determination of GLP-1R bound with small molecule agonists, expecting to obtain binding site details.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cryo-EM structures revealed the interaction mechanism between small-molecule agonists and GLP-1R, identifying key factors W33 and R380.

references.relevance: Describes cryo-EM structure analysis of GLP-1R agonist complexes for drug optimization.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FBDD relies on three-dimensional structural information of target proteins. [Gap] Structural elucidation of suitable targets for FBDD using traditional crystal diffraction is challenging. [Decision] To guide FBDD drug discovery, chose to use Cryo-EM to perform complex structure analysis of β-galactosidase with small molecules, expecting to obtain binding pocket details.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cryo-EM clearly displayed the small-molecule structure of the binding site and conformational changes of the active site protein.

references.relevance: First exploration of cryo-EM application to FBDD with Bgal system.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FBDD technology is suitable for proteins with multiple active sites and high molecular mass. [Gap] Applicability of cryo-EM in FBDD for cancer-related kinases needed demonstration. [Decision] To further demonstrate applicability, chose to use Cryo-EM to perform structural analysis of PKM2 with small-molecule fragments, expecting to obtain complex structures.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Multiple compound complex structures with PKM2 were determined, demonstrating applicability of cryo-EM in FBDD.

references.relevance: Demonstrates cryo-EM applicability in FBDD with PKM2 system.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PROTAC drugs induce degradation of target proteins via ubiquitin-proteasome system. [Gap] Mechanism of induced degradation for specific drugs like indisulam needed structural elucidation. [Decision] To resolve anticancer mechanism, chose to use Cryo-EM combined with X-ray crystallography to perform structure analysis of DCAF15-DDB1-RBM39 complex, expecting to obtain degradation mechanism.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Electron microscope structure showed indisulam specifically binds to RBM39 and promotes interaction leading to cancer cell death.

references.relevance: Combined X-ray and cryo-EM to resolve anticancer mechanism of indisulam.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SARS-CoV-2 infection involves S protein binding to ACE2. [Gap] Rapid antibody development requires key epitope information without isolating monoclonal antibodies. [Decision] To shorten screening time, chose to use Cryo-EM combined with next-generation sequencing to perform complex structure analysis of S-ACE2-antibody, expecting to obtain epitope models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Structural information provided guidance for antibody screening and structure optimization against mutant strains.

references.relevance: Describes cryo-EM role in SARS-CoV-2 antibody drug development and epitope discovery.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AI-based protein structure prediction methods are expected to impact drug discovery. [Gap] Cryo-EM maps sometimes at medium resolution need better model fitting. [Decision] To obtain sophisticated models, chose to use AlphaFold2 to perform structure prediction integrated with cryo-EM on nuclear pore complex, expecting to obtain fitted models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: AlphaFold-produced template solved phasing problems and guided model fitting of the CR map.

references.citation: Fontana et al. [116], Lupas et al. [115]
references.relevance: Reports integrative cryo-EM and AlphaFold for structure determination.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Automation, throughput and resolution level are obstacles restricting cryo-EM application. [Gap] Data collection and processing need optimization to minimize human intervention. [Decision] To solve path planning and particle picking problems, chose to use Topaz and cryoRL to perform data processing and collection optimization, expecting to obtain improved signal-to-noise ratio.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Topaz showed merits in small, nonglobal and asymmetric particles and improving signal-to-noise ratio.

references.relevance: Describes Topaz as efficient particle-picking program based on neural networks.

resolution.interpretation: Highest resolution of proteins analyzed by cryo-EM reached atomic resolution.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved as cryo-EM has reached 1.2 Å resolution, facilitating drug discovery as overviewed.
